title: Road to AGI: A Road with Curves
icon_list_heading: AI Communicator on YouTube
text_editor_paragraphs: Progress in Artificial Intelligence is often perceived as an exponential curve that only accelerates into an unpredictable chaos of daily headlines. The reality is more complex—and more fascinating. AI advances in a series of overlapping waves, where each new capability emerges, accelerates, and eventually hits a ceiling. Where are we really on this winding path? Why do today’s models seem to stall at certain tasks while excelling at others? And most importantly, what breakthroughs still lie ahead on the road to true AGI?
Discover exactly where we stand on the curve today, which ceilings we are currently hitting, and which critical pieces are still missing to unlock the next major stage of artificial intelligence.

headings: Senior Cloud Developer Advocate at Google
title: The future of Software Development in the age of AI
icon_list_heading: Senior Cloud Developer Advocate at Google
text_editor_paragraphs: In a time of rapid change where AI is a board-level conversation on it’s impact on software development. How do developers respond? We will explore why the future is about giving developers smarter tools to build smarter apps on smarter platforms.   Discover the fundamental shifts that will empower developers to: focus on simplicity, experiment quickly, build agentic solutions, shift down responsibilities through platform engineering, and use AI assistance. Join Abdel Sghiouar, for a look into the future of our industry.

This session presents a concrete case where AI and emerging technologies delivers measurable business value through close collaboration between Business teams in S2P and Finance and IT, guided by our way of working which is Dynamic Shared Ownership (DSO).

SheAI explores why diverse talent isn’t optional and the unique value women bring to AI deployment. Learn which roles are in demand, essential skills from AI literacy to ethical design, and a concrete roadmap to pivot into this AI-first landscape.

text_editor_paragraphs: While the market talks about products and applications, the next generation of deep-tech AI is being developed in Catalan laboratories. We will explore how the country’s research system generates these technologies and what mechanisms allow them to be transformed into real-world impact.

title: Demystifying Intelligence in AI Agents
text_editor_paragraphs: Connect strategy and engineering to explain how modern agentic systems are built. After a high‑level view of deploying secure, sovereign AI, we dive into the technical foundations: transformers, attention, model internals, and the math behind large language models. Participants walk through the full GPT‑2 architecture and build it from scratch in Python, gaining a fundamental understanding of how agents work and how to build them responsibly.

headings: Google Developer Expert Web Technologies - Google Developers Group Spain
title: Getting Started with Google Antigravity
icon_list_heading: Google Developer Expert Web Technologies - Google Developers Group Spain
text_editor_paragraphs: In this codelab, you will learn about Google Antigravity (referred to as Antigravity for the rest of the document), an agentic development platform, evolving the IDE into the agent-first era. Unlike standard coding assistants that just autocomplete lines, Antigravity provides a “Mission Control” for managing autonomous agents that can plan, code, and even browse the web to help you build. Antigravity is designed as an agent-first platform. It presupposes that the AI is not just a tool for writing code but an autonomous actor capable of planning, executing, validating, and iterating on complex engineering tasks with minimal human intervention.

headings: Firmware Engineer & Developer Advocate at Arduino
title: UNO Q Robot Dog: Dual-Brain Control in Action
icon_list_heading: Firmware Engineer & Developer Advocate at Arduino
text_editor_paragraphs: Discover advanced robotics with Arduino UNO Q through a four-legged robot dog remotely controlled via dual-band Wi-Fi. UNO Q’s dual-brain architecture combines MCU real-time motor control with MPU high-level processing and sensor fusion, enabling smooth movement and responsive interaction in a compact, connected platform.

title: When 3rd-Parties Invade Your Domain: Protect Your Business from External Chaos
icon_list_heading: Backend developer at Seat Code
text_editor_paragraphs: In this talk, we will explore the rapid journey that has brought us here: from early models like GPT-2 and GPT-3, to the global impact of ChatGPT and the current era of multimodal models. We will see how, in just a few years, artificial intelligence has evolved from merely understanding and generating text to reasoning, seeing, hearing, and acting—paving the way for a new paradigm of more autonomous and adaptive systems.

title: The Orbital Reckoning: Why the next balance of Power will be decided in Space
icon_list_heading: CEO & Founder at Space DOTS
text_editor_paragraphs: The world is entering an orbital era where deterrence, infrastructure, and national resilience are shaped by what happens above Earth. As a European founder building in this domain, I’ll reveal the real forces unfolding in orbit – silent interference, environmental volatility, and new power dynamics – and share the strategic shifts Europe must make now to stay competitive in a rapidly weaponised space environment.

headings: Head of IT Cyber Detection at Boehringer Ingelheim
Head of Cyber Prevention at Boehringer Ingelheim
title: In the spotlight: Know your potential adversaries and strengthen your defenses
icon_list_heading: Head of IT Cyber Detection at Boehringer Ingelheim
text_editor_paragraphs: The session shows how to identify and profile adversaries by analyzing tactics and patterns, turn intelligence into Adversary Emulation scenarios, and run realistic simulations. It allows you to measure detections, uncover gaps, validate resilience, and assess defenses against advanced attacks—including EDR evasions—with a threat-informed approach.

title: R&D in the era of AI
icon_list_heading: Senior Research Engineer at Huawei
text_editor_paragraphs: AI is causing a technological revolution in multiple fields. In this talk, we will see how it is changing R&D, showing AI applied to real industrial problems and introducing different ways of using it beyond chatbots. The talk will be pedagogic in nature, assuming no previous knowledge of AI, and will also present potential dangers and limitations of current AI models.

headings: HyperX Category and Biz. Development Manager at HP
title: Success.exe: The discipline behind performance
icon_list_heading: HyperX Category and Biz. Development Manager at HP
text_editor_paragraphs: In an era of instant gratification, peak performance is often misunderstood as talent—or luck. In reality, sustained success is rarely accidental; it is the result of deliberate structure and disciplined execution. In this opening session at Talent Arena, we will break down the core variables behind high performance: Talent, Willingness, and Time. From the “garage mindset” that shaped HP’s early culture to the relentless precision of athletes and esports legends like Stephen Curry or Daigo Umehara, we will explore what separates potential from mastery. Through real examples, we will challenge the myth of overnight success and examine why discipline—not inspiration—is the true operating system behind long-term achievement. Attendees will leave with a clearer framework to rethink performance in a tech-driven world where execution is the ultimate competitive advantage.

title: When AI Joins the Team: New Rules of the Game
text_editor_paragraphs: 1. Víctor Agramunt (Moderator) – Data & AI Manager at Seidor
2. Xavier Roig Gràcia – Chief Information Officer at Grupo Familia Torres
3. Iñaki Suárez – Chief Technology Officer at Basque AI Center (BAIC)
4. Fernando Marín Gurrea – Head of the AI Office at Barcelona Innovació i Tecnologia – Ajuntament de Barcelona
5. Xavier Manzanera – Head of Digital Office, Data & AI at Esteve

AI is changing the rules of the game in technology projects and teams, but not everyone is integrating it in the same way or achieving the same results. What makes the difference? This session isn’t about theories—we want to share real experiences from different organizations to help us understand: what’s working and what’s not, what changes need to be addressed, how leadership evolves when AI becomes part of the team’s daily work, and how decision-making shifts. Don’t miss it.

Faced with threats such as drones and cyberattacks, it integrates cognitive superiority, autonomous operations, and maximum cyber resilience; to anticipate risks, coordinate intelligent systems, and ensure operational continuity, strengthening institutional response and laying the groundwork for security and defense solutions.

title: Building the future in Rust
icon_list_heading: Executive Director & CEO of Rust
text_editor_paragraphs: Rust is one of the most loved and wanted languages today. Its memory safety, speed, and lack of a garbage collector make it ideal for safety-critical automotive, aerospace, robotics, cloud, and embedded systems. It’s also gaining traction for production AI inference, especially high-throughput LLMs and edge deployments. Rust offers a clear path to modernize legacy systems, improve safety, and build the next generation of reliable software.

headings: CEO and Presenter of Hackers' Land at Triskel Security
title: Radical privacy: small actions with big impact
icon_list_heading: CEO and Presenter of Hackers' Land at Triskel Security
text_editor_paragraphs: The abuse of our personal data and privacy is no longer the exception, but the norm. Every day, we see headlines about security breaches exposing personal information, social networks turning our daily lives into a product, government agencies taking surveillance to the extreme, and companies profiting from every click we make. Our information is collected, stored, and exploited in ways we often don’t even realize. What’s worrying is that the trend is only heading upward, with increasingly serious impacts on our privacy and personal security. In this talk, we will highlight many of the ways and methods through which we unknowingly hand over our information, and we will explore how to apply operational security (OPSEC) principles to our everyday digital lives. We’ll discuss real changes, as well as methodologies, strategies, and small tricks that anyone can adopt to reduce their digital footprint, minimize risks, and regain some control over their online identity.

headings: Director of Engineering at Edge Impulse
title: From Reflex to Reason: The Engineering Journey to Physical AI
icon_list_heading: Director of Engineering at Edge Impulse
text_editor_paragraphs: In 2019, “Edge AI” was a feat of magic: fitting a simple keyword spotter onto a microcontroller. Today, in 2026, we are running 11-billion-parameter Vision-Language Models (VLMs) on pocket-sized silicon. But how did we get here, and where is the “intelligence of everything” actually going?

title: Technology with purpose: Making music truly inclusive
icon_list_heading: Robotics Software Engineer at ERNI
text_editor_paragraphs: Technology takes on its deepest meaning when it serves people. Guided by this belief, ERNI, ABB and Fundación SIFU created an innovative project that brings music within reach of those who once thought it impossible. By combining robotics, artificial intelligence and human creativity, a young pianist with cerebral palsy, Joel Bueno, can now play a traditional piano using only his eyes, turning engineering into a powerful symbol of inclusion and emotion.

title: You Play the CTF. We Play Defense
text_editor_paragraphs: In this session, you’ll use your own laptop to jump into a hands‑on CTF, playing as a pentester while you explore and attack, just like a real red teamer. Then the game stops, and the blue team takes over to show you what your actions look like from the defender’s side: the alerts, the signals, and how security teams respond in real environments. It’s not just a CTF. It’s you playing the attack, and us revealing the defense in real time.

headings: Head of Developer Marketing at Qualcomm
title: On‑Device LLMs with Genie 2: Building a Chat App Using Qualcomm® AI Hub Apps
icon_list_heading: Head of Developer Marketing at Qualcomm
text_editor_paragraphs: Join this hands-on workshop and learn how to run large language models fully on-device with the Qualcomm® AI Hub™ Apps. You’ll use a pretrained LLM from Qualcomm AI Hub Models to build and run a C++ ChatApp optimized to run on the Qualcomm® Hexagon™ NPU.

icon_list_heading: Google Developer Expert Web Technologies - Google Developers Group Spain

title: From legacy CI/CD to modern software delivery
icon_list_heading: Solutions Engineer at GitHub
text_editor_paragraphs: A deep dive into authoring, refactoring, and troubleshooting CI/CD pipelines, supported by GitHub Copilot and GitHub Advanced Security. We’ll show how to migrate legacy pipelines powered by bash scripts into clear, reusable workflow components, implement new pipeline jobs safely, and eliminate common execution errors through precise permissions, deterministic steps, and disciplined artifact handling. A live demo will demonstrate interpreting Actions logs, isolating failing steps, and harnessing Copilot for targeted fixes. We’ll bake in supply chain security by enforcing least‑privilege workflow permissions, pinning external actions, and running security checks from GitHub Advanced Security as part of every change. Attendees will leave with repeatable patterns to accelerate delivery while raising the security and reliability bar across repositories.

title: This is the future: programming a humanoid robot
icon_list_heading: Digital tech communicator & Business Owner
text_editor_paragraphs: In this talk I will show you how a humanoid robot works in 2026, how it is programmed, what can be done with it, what advantages artificial intelligence gives us, and where all this is heading.

headings: Associate Professor at ESADE and Board Member at Medidedalia
title: Inside the Filter Bubble: Rethinking Recommendations in the GenAI Era
icon_list_heading: Associate Professor at ESADE and Board Member at Medidedalia
text_editor_paragraphs: As generative AI becomes the primary interface to information, recommendation systems are quietly transforming into engines of interpretation. Instead of simply filtering content, GenAI systems increasingly define how knowledge is framed, explained, and contextualized—creating personalized realities at scale.

This talk explores the emergence of a new kind of filter bubble, one rooted not in what is shown, but in how meaning is constructed. It examines the societal, cultural, and economic implications of AI systems that adapt narratives to individual users, potentially narrowing perspectives while appearing more helpful than ever.

By reframing recommendation systems as cognitive infrastructure, the session argues for a shift in design philosophy—from maximizing engagement to preserving pluralism, serendipity, and trust. The talk concludes by outlining how industry leaders can ensure that GenAI-powered recommendations expand human understanding rather than constrain it.

title: Beyond Agentic AI: Designing the Future of Healthcare with Women in STEM

At AstraZeneca, science, data and technology drive AI-enabled healthcare. We’re advancing from generative AI (faster thinking/learning) to agentic AI (acting across complex systems) across discovery, development, evidence and patient care. The Hub blends scientific rigor, digital expertise and responsible AI, builds the future workforce,especially women in STEM, and offers frontier opportunities, non-linear careers and purpose-led impact.

headings: Former Microsoft Chief Security Advisor and Cybersecurity and Crisis leader
title: SAFER by Design: Rewriting the rule book
icon_list_heading: Former Microsoft Chief Security Advisor and Cybersecurity and Crisis leader
text_editor_paragraphs: What if ‘secure by design’ was just the beginning, but not nearly enough? In today’s fast-paced world, where design decisions can affect millions, safety must be our guiding principle. In this high-tempo keynote, Sarah Armstrong-Smith introduces the SAFER by design framework: Secure, Accessible, Functional, Environmental, Reliable. This isn’t a checklist – SAFER represents a transformative mindset shift for everyone shaping the future of tech, from engineers to execs. Attendees will gain actionable insights to embed SAFER principles into every stage of product development, driving innovation and resilience. By embracing SAFER by design, we raise the bar for industry standards and deliver lasting positive change.

headings: Founder & Studio Director at Highball Animation
title: Denshattack: How to Approach the Animation of a Crazy Video Game
icon_list_heading: Founder & Studio Director at Highball Animation
text_editor_paragraphs: Join Highball Animation in collaboration with Undercoders for a look at how we brought Denshattack to life. We’re going to walk you through our animation process, starting with how we move from first sketches to the final high-speed polish without losing efficiency. We’ll also share our favorite camera tricks and show you how we use framing to make the game feel snappy and responsive for the player. Finally, we’ll talk about our approach to cinematics and how we mix different styles and techniques to make every moment of the game stand out. Come see how we combine a solid workflow with creativity to make even our wildest ideas work.

headings: Professor at Universidad Europea, Software Engineer
title: From Prompt to Production: Rapid Prototyping of AI Applications with Firebase Studio
icon_list_heading: Professor at Universidad Europea, Software Engineer
text_editor_paragraphs: Turn a natural language prompt into a production-ready AI web application, live. This session shows how the Firebase Studio Application Prototyping agent generates the initial structure of a complete Next.js project, connects it to Firebase services, and uses the Gemini-powered Code Assist to perform instant refinements, testing, and one-click deployments. You’ll be ready to launch your own idea in minutes, all from the browser.

headings: Co-President and Executive Director at LawZero
title: Our Roadmap to Safe AI
icon_list_heading: Co-President and Executive Director at LawZero
text_editor_paragraphs: As AI models become increasingly capable, the need to ensure that these models are aligned with our objectives and that their behaviour does not contravene our fundamental norms becomes more and more crucial.  With increasing frequency, we are seeing cases of LLMs dispaying unwanted behaviours, such as deception, excessive sycophancy and self-preservation.  At LawZero, we are exploring and developing alternative methodologies to current LLM structures in order to train advanced AI models that are more transparent, reliable and aligned.  We want to dispel the myth that increased AI safety can only come at the expense of advancing capabilities.

title: Why soft skills are becoming increasingly important in a digital-first world
text_editor_paragraphs: As technology, systems, and automation accelerate execution, the real challenge shifts from tools to people. This session explores why communication, decision-making, trust, and alignment have become the hardest and most critical skills for leaders and teams in a digital-first environment.

title: AI + Human Judgment: How to Make HR More Efficient Without Losing Its Soul
text_editor_paragraphs: Showcase real and practical examples of how AI is already supporting HR teams. Break the fear of AI as a replacement, positioning it as an enabler. Reposition HR as a strategic partner rather than an administrative function. Provide actionable takeaways that attendees can apply in their organizations.

title: Decode Yourself: Discover Your DISC Style and Improve How You Work
text_editor_paragraphs: The DISC model identifies four behavioral styles that influence how we make decisions, communicate, and face challenges. Understanding them improves teamwork, professional effectiveness, and interpersonal relationships, helping individuals gain better self-awareness and respond appropriately in any situation. A workshop designed to help participants decode themselves through DISC.

title: IKIGAI: The art of following your purpose in the age of AI
icon_list_heading: Writer and lecturer
text_editor_paragraphs: Francesc Miralles, co-author of the groundbreaking book praised by Oprah Winfrey, translated into 70 languages with 6 million copies sold, will talk about the purpose in work and life according to this fascinating and inspiring Japanese concept. To do so, we’ll learn how the four circles of ikigai work, as well as the tools we have at our disposal to discover and enhance our life’s mission in the age of AI.

icon_list_heading: International winner NASA's Lunar Recycle Challenge

In this inspiring talk, mining engineer and the only international winner of NASA’s lunar challenge, María Jesús Puerta, shares the story of Esperanza: a digital twin capable of recycling waste on the Moon. A true story showing that effort and technology can take us farther than we ever imagined.

headings: Software Engineer and Programming Content Creator
title: Programming is dead. Long live programming!
icon_list_heading: Software Engineer and Programming Content Creator
text_editor_paragraphs: Programming isn’t dying, it’s just transforming. AI has arrived to stay, shaking the foundations of software development and opening up a range of opportunities and new technologies. Smarter web applications, LLMs running in your browser, new APIs like WebGPU to harness your GPU, and unprecedented ways to elevate the user experience. All with live examples. Because knowing how to program is what will make the difference between creating amazing things or blindly trusting that AI is never wrong. (Spoiler: it is.)

title: Aubac: Gamify the modification of files
icon_list_heading: Student at UB
text_editor_paragraphs: This talk presents Aubac, an experimental video game that transforms the player’s file system into the primary space for interaction. Through the use of APIs like System.IO, real-time change detection, and data manipulation, the game transforms everyday actions such as renaming, moving, or modifying files into playable mechanics. The technical decisions behind this approach will be explored, along with the challenges of working with real files.

headings: Tech Co-Founder & Staff Software Engineer at ReactJS
title: Micro-Frontend & Me, a love story
icon_list_heading: Tech Co-Founder & Staff Software Engineer at ReactJS
text_editor_paragraphs: I always thought Micro-frontends were stupid, unproven tech, and a fad. After speaking with industry leaders on my podcast about how they implemented it at scale, I realized that it might actually solve some issues we are having in our company. This talk focuses on the journey of implementing micro-frontends, from trade-offs, implementation, and choosing the right tools for the job.

title: Designing architecture with EventStorming and Bounded Contexts
icon_list_heading: Principal Engineer at N26
text_editor_paragraphs: Are you tired of being blocked by other teams or working in a large, shared codebase where every change feels risky? Learn how to evolve a tangled monolith—or a distributed monolith—into a clean, scalable distributed architecture. This talk will show you how to identify Bounded Contexts using a Big Picture EventStorming workshop, in close collaboration with Domain Experts, to uncover boundaries between subsystems.

icon_list_heading: Director, Product Software Engineering at Wolters Kluwer
text_editor_paragraphs: In today’s large-scale and highly distributed enterprise environments, organizations must build software that is consistent, scalable, and tightly aligned with business rules and operational objectives. Enterprise Spec Driven Development introduces a strategic approach in which formal, evolving, and shared specifications become the single source of truth guiding the design, development, and lifecycle of modern enterprise solutions.

This session explores the paradigm shift redefining the labor market and the urgent need for adaptation. Learn to move beyond the hype and integrate AI as a strategic co-pilot. Key Takeaways: The impact of AI on market agility. Redefining productivity in the digital era. Frameworks for effective technology adoption.

title: Immersive Interview Experience: Live a Real Interview
text_editor_paragraphs: The job interview is a demanding process that requires preparation and strategy. Through a hands-on, “training mode” experience, participants can practice, improve their pitch, and gain confidence to perform at their best in a real interview. In this workshop, you will find the most realistic simulator to help you avoid mistakes in an actual job interview.

icon_list_heading: CEO at Industry-Academia Innovation College, National Taiwan University of Science and Technology
text_editor_paragraphs: Global semiconductor talent development is increasingly vital as supply chains globalize and technology cycles accelerate. This abstract examines the global talent history and future strategies for building a skilled workforce through education, industry–academia partnerships and international collaboration which enables knowledge exchange while addressing talent shortages across regions.

icon_list_heading: CEO of World2Meet
text_editor_paragraphs: Travel is one of the most desired experiences and is undergoing transformation thanks to technology and Digital Experience Design. With the customer at the center and personalized phygital experiences, World2Meet will present in this keynote its vision and The New Travel Project, aimed at redefining the design, marketing, and travel experience, generating value for travelers, partners, and destinations.

text_editor_paragraphs: After a brief and dynamic introduction to LLMs and their key uses, you will face several challenges designed to test your ingenuity and creativity, competing with other participants and showing your ability to go beyond the machine. Ready to take on the challenge? Get ready to challenge AI, amaze your audience, and remember that human creativity still makes the difference.

title: Beyond the Model: Agentic Orchestration as the New AI Architecture
icon_list_heading: Chief AI Officer (CAIO) at Thinkia
text_editor_paragraphs: This session examines the shift from standalone models to orchestrated multi-agent systems. We explore how structured memory, task decomposition, and small specialized models redefine scalability and autonomy. Designed for leaders, it provides a strategic framework to understand how agentic systems impact enterprise architecture, operational efficiency, and long-term competitiveness in the new era of AI deployments.

headings: AI Business Solutions Lead at Zurich
AI Governance Tech Lead at Zurich
title: From Hype to Harmony: Implementing Agentic AI Responsibly At Scale
icon_list_heading: AI Business Solutions Lead at Zurich
text_editor_paragraphs: AI agents are transforming work at Zurich—making processes faster, smarter, and more efficient. From automating tasks to enabling complex decisions, they deliver real value. The next wave, Agentic AI, brings autonomy and big opportunities—but also risks. Success means innovation with trust: safety, transparency, and accountability from day one.

title: Semiconductors: The Cornerstone of an AI-Driven Future
icon_list_heading: Vice President of Digitimes
text_editor_paragraphs: In this session, I will first review the semiconductor market and the industry’s evolution during the tech paradigm shift. Then, I will introduce the development of AI stacks following the launch of ChatGPT and analyze the dominant forces in the AI ecosystem and their semiconductor footprints. Lastly, I will depict the future world empowered by AI and robots, along with the emerging demand and challenges for semiconductors.

title: From response to preparedness: AI & and Data at the service of people and society
text_editor_paragraphs: The world is increasingly suffering from natural disasters attributed to climate change. Frequent flooding is not only happening anymore in the global south, but also in Europe and the US, with the resulting disruption and fatal victims. Today, the fight against such disasters focuses mostly on responding once the disaster has happened (Response). While this is of crucial importance, it is equally important to focus on preparedness. Countries that are prepared can be more efficient in the response phase, reducing the amount of fatal victims, as well as the economic and societal disruption.

In this panel, we will discuss how to be prepared for natural disasters, how AI & data plays a key role, and what challenges hinder preparedness. The panel will also show concrete and real examples of preparedness, provide an open source data platform, and announce the Mobile Resilient Initiative, a global community for preparedness.

title: Innovative and Technological Talent: The Key Role of Vocational Education and Training
text_editor_paragraphs: 1. Antonio Saravia – Deputy Director for the Digital Economy at Red.es (Moderator)

This panel will examine how, in a labor market that demands profiles capable of driving the digital and green transition, vocational education and training (VET) educates over 1,200,000 students with programs aligned with the productive sector. It will explain how, thanks to collaboration among stakeholders and public-private initiatives, key needs and profiles are identified. Today, VET is the main source of talent for many technology companies.

title: The Missing Structure in Most Tech Teams: Building a Strong Product Trio
icon_list_heading: Product Owner at SEAT CODE
text_editor_paragraphs: Tech teams are expected to move fast and act autonomously. But when Product, Design and Tech don’t truly operate as a unit, delivery slows down and decisions get revisited. The Product Trio isn’t just a discovery practice — it’s a structural choice that impacts speed, clarity and real autonomy. In this roundtable, we’ll discuss about what changes when the trio is strong — and what quietly breaks when it isn’t.

title: Hack Your CV: Key Tips to Optimize Your CV and Beat AI Filters
text_editor_paragraphs: In today’s recruitment processes, many résumés are automatically filtered before reaching human hands. Understanding how these systems work is key to improving job opportunities. This workshop provides practical tools and strategies to adapt your CV to AI filters, aiming especially to empower unemployed individuals, people with diverse profiles, or those in vulnerable situations.

headings: Content creator at LopezNorman44 and El Ganxo
title: Gaming, Language and Communities
icon_list_heading: Content creator at LopezNorman44 and El Ganxo
text_editor_paragraphs: A conversation between content creator LopezNorman44 and video game developer Unai Estavillo from Coolom Games (developers ofDating Killmulator) about building communities in the digital environment around video games.

headings: Deputy Director-General, DG CONNECT, European Commission
title: What can I do to convince you to get a job in cybersecurity?
icon_list_heading: Deputy Director-General, DG CONNECT, European Commission
text_editor_paragraphs: Why do so many talented people excel in international cybersecurity competitions, yet never choose a career in cybersecurity? Is it because they don’t apply – or because jobs aren’t attractive enough? Or are we trapped by outdated ideas about who these roles are for and what skills they require?
This session challenges the myths behind the cybersecurity skills shortage and shows how cybersecurity can become an opportunity for professionals far beyond IT.

title: AI Changed the Rules for Teamwork – Are You Ready?
icon_list_heading: AI Evangelist at Atlassian
text_editor_paragraphs: AI writes code in parallel, discovers and fixes problems in production, and incorporates customer feedback in real-time. Using agentic AI in software development is not just adding a new tool to your workflow like Git or CI/CD; it redefines how developers get work done. If agents step in as our technical advisors, coaches, and strategic partners, how will humans work together? Do we still need traditional teams? Will we plan our work together? Is this the end of the cross-functional team? Let’s throw out the crystal ball and see what’s possible today and how teams are already transforming their ways of working. One thing is clear: we must recalibrate how we work together.

title: AI in a supercomputing world: the next scientific frontier
icon_list_heading: Associate director of BSC
text_editor_paragraphs: Artificial Intelligence and High-Performance Computing are converging to redefine the way science is conceived, executed, and applied. This fusion is enabling breakthroughs across domains: from building Earth Twins to understand and mitigate climate change, to developing sovereign chip designs tailored for AI, and advancing dual-use technologies that balance innovation with responsibility. European Large Language Models are reshaping how multilingual societies engage with knowledge, while new computational methods are opening frontiers in social sciences, medicine, and engineering alike. This new era is not just accelerating discovery; it is evolving the very scientific method toward a future where AI-driven simulations, transparent data practices, and ethical design are central pillars of progress. In this talk, we will do a walkthrough all these topics with sparks from the work that the Barcelona Supercomputing Center has been leading wordlwide

headings: AI Expert & Data Scientist at Next Education
title: Renaissance 2.0: Against the Fragmented Mind
icon_list_heading: AI Expert & Data Scientist at Next Education
text_editor_paragraphs: For centuries, humanity progressed thanks to hybrid minds—those that combined science, art, and philosophy without artificial divisions. Today, in the age of artificial intelligence, we face the same challenge: will we continue to see creativity and logic as separate worlds? AI is blurring these boundaries, and the future belongs to those who embrace the convergence of technology and humanity. It is time to revive the spirit of the Renaissance, where critical thinking, imagination, and technical knowledge merge to create something truly transcendent.

title: Princess of Girona Inspires: Building Digital Talent with Impact and Values
text_editor_paragraphs: 1. Elisenda Bou-Balust – Co-Founder & CEO, Cala AI
2. Ignasi Belda – Department Director, Banco de España
3. Pau García-Milà – Co-founder & Co-CEO, Founderz Business School (Moderator)
4. Ingrid Aznar Juncà – Awards Director, Princess of Girona Foundation

How do you build a technological career with real impact? Which decisions make a difference when you’re just starting out? What role do ethics, entrepreneurship, and leadership play in the development of digital talent?

In this inspiring conversation, Ignasi Belda, Pau García-Milà, and Elisenda Bou—awardees of theFundació Princesa de Girona—will share the stage to reflect on how to grow professionally in the tech sector from very diverse perspectives and experiences.

Through an open and approachable dialogue, they will address key topics such as starting a career, decision-making in moments of uncertainty, the value of failure, entrepreneurship as a driver of change, responsibility in technological development, and the importance of incorporating ethical and human criteria into innovation.
An honest and motivating conversation that aims to convey that there is no single path to success, but there are shared values that can make a difference.

headings: Customer Satisfaction, Quality & Transformation Vice President at Schneider Electric

icon_list_heading: Customer Satisfaction, Quality & Transformation Vice President at Schneider Electric
text_editor_paragraphs: What makes someone stand out in data or AI? Spoiler: it’s not mastering models, dashboards, or the latest technologies. Curiosity is your greatest asset. Asking better questions and understanding context is what turns data into real knowledge. In this session, we’ll talk about how you can become the kind of professional who helps others make smarter decisions and creates meaningful impact. Because data is cool… but knowledge is the end game.

headings: Head of Experience Design & Research at NTT DATA
title: Autonomous Business: Are We Ready for AI to Make Decisions?
icon_list_heading: Head of Experience Design & Research at NTT DATA
text_editor_paragraphs: Autonomous Business is the next paradigm following the digital transformation of organizations. Agentic AI takes a central role and promises efficiency, faster time-to-market, and value creation at levels we never imagined. But for these promises to be fulfilled, companies and organizations must have both their technology and their people prepared. Is yours ready?

text_editor_paragraphs: While developing HeartWeaver – currently featured in Steam Next Fest with a free demo – our primary challenge was implementing systems that had to be easy to use for non-developers but also scalable and modifiable, since game development is an iterative journey to “find the fun”.

In this talk, you will be able to know more about the technical challenges while developing systems that do not come out of the box in Unreal Engine: gravity switch mechanics, multi-format dialogues with localisation, a rail-based static camera system with custom framing and a multi-slot save system.

This workshop provides practical tools for managing interviews and communication with confidence, helping neurodivergent and diverse individuals showcase their talent, strengthen self-awareness, and enter the tech sector with greater assurance.

title: Building with Open-Source AI: From Theory to Practice
text_editor_paragraphs: Are you bored with AI talks that only focus on trends? Discover the initiative of the MTDFP Open Source AI Community, showcasing the technical aspects of real-world use cases. Solutions to concrete challenges and applications with social impact. You’ll see how the community is collaborating to solve real problems using open tools, and how you can get involved, collaborate, and contribute. Come to this talk if you want to be part of the change.

headings: Chief Software Officer at Openchip & Software Technologies SL
title: AI and Europe’s Digital Destiny: Building Sovereignty through Values
icon_list_heading: Chief Software Officer at Openchip & Software Technologies SL
text_editor_paragraphs: As artificial intelligence becomes the backbone of the digital century, Europe faces a decisive choice: allow its sovereignty to erode or build it on its own terms. By federating AI infrastructure, securing control over data and compute, and embedding safety, sustainability, and human dignity into technology itself, we can turn values into strategic advantage. This keynote calls for a Europe that leads not through domination, but through example, positioning trust, collaboration, and environmental responsibility as the foundations of a sovereign European AI ecosystem.

title: It’s not just another bot: it’s an inspirational agent for the shopping of the future
icon_list_heading: Technical Architect at Vueling
text_editor_paragraphs: The next generation of shopping will go beyond web and apps: it will live in an interoperable agentic ecosystem. We introduce Vueling’s Inspirational Agent, an agentic architecture with MCPs and MCPApps, integrable into owned channels or external environments. We explain context design, coordination, and tool-calling over core systems, ensuring observability, security, governance, and controlled costs—laying the foundation today for the agentic world of tomorrow.

headings: Cecil and Ida Green Professor of Physics
title: New Quantum Materials for Quantum 2.0
icon_list_heading: Cecil and Ida Green Professor of Physics
text_editor_paragraphs: The birth of Quantum Physics in the early 20th century gave rise to all modern technology. This first “Quantum Revolution” made use of the most basic concepts in quantum physics. In the coming decades, a second quantum revolution (Quantum 2.0), using much more advanced quantum physics, will dramatically change the way we do science and technology, with dramatic implications for the economy and national security. In his talk, Prof. Jarillo-Herrero will discuss a new generation of quantum materials with the potential to revolutionize quantum simulations, quantum sensing, and AI applications

From day-one setup – where you review every line – through guardrails and context management, to autonomous agents that can own your repo while you tackle the problems no one’s solved yet.

title: Closing the Velocity Gap: Syncing AI and Innovation with Healthcare Adoption

AI and digital technologies are advancing at unprecedented speed, yet healthcare adoption often lags due to regulatory, cultural, and operational constraints. This talk explores the “velocity gap” between innovation and real-world impact, drawing on experience from Roche to share practical insights on governance, cross-functional collaboration, and execution strategies that turn AI into sustainable value for patients and healthcare professionals

Damm drives a Data and AI strategy based on the IAmm Hub, created to accelerate projects and generate value. Its key pillars are Data Governance, essential for ensuring quality and security, and a flexible, robust technology platform. The talk will showcase the current status, key learnings, and challenges in scaling a reliable and adaptable ecosystem.

headings: Software Engineer and Programming Content Creator
title: Creating a Real Product with AI: Professional Vibe Coding for Developers
icon_list_heading: Software Engineer and Programming Content Creator
text_editor_paragraphs: In this workshop we will build a functional product from scratch, using artificial intelligence tools as real engineering accelerators.This is not about asking a model for code and copying it. It’s about designing, deciding, and building better.

During the session we will work with tools such as Stitch to structure and rapidly prototype the experience, Claude Code and Open Code to iterate on architecture and business logic, and Antigravity to explore agent-assisted workflows. We will also see how to run models directly in the browser using ONNX, integrating AI capabilities into the product itself without relying exclusively on the backend.
Throughout the process we will cover:
Defining architecture with AI support without delegating technical judgment
Validating and refactoring model-generated code
Designing AI-powered user experiences
Integrating models in the frontend and understanding their real limitations
What changes in the role of the senior developer when code generation is almost instantaneous?
AI does not replace the developer. It multiplies them — but only if they know how to use it.

This workshop is aimed at experienced developers who want to understand how to integrate AI into their professional workflow, improve their delivery capacity, and redefine their value in the era of intelligent assistants.

title: Talent and Disability: Breaking Barriers and Driving Inclusion in the AI Era

headings: Principal Data & AI Engineer at ERNI
title: Building complex code-defined AI agents that execute robotic actions
icon_list_heading: Principal Data & AI Engineer at ERNI
text_editor_paragraphs: In this session, we will break open the black box of language model–based agents (LLMs) to understand how they are built and connected. We will also explore how these agents can go beyond simply answering questions with text, performing real-world actions that are automatically executed by a robotic arm.

headings: Physicist, science communicator and creator of the podcast "Quantum Fracture"
title: Quantum Sensors: The Technology That Measures the Impossible
icon_list_heading: Physicist, science communicator and creator of the podcast "Quantum Fracture"
text_editor_paragraphs: When people talk about quantum technology, almost all the attention goes to quantum computers, presented as the great revolution of the future. But they are not the only one, nor necessarily the most influential. This talk focuses on a silent revolution already underway: quantum sensors. Through atomic clocks and SQUIDs, it combines scientific explanation with real-world anecdotes to show how quantum physics has moved from theory to measuring the impossible.

headings: AI Expert at Banco de España
AI Expert at Banco de España
title: Another bot? No: an architecture
icon_list_heading: AI Expert at Banco de España
text_editor_paragraphs: As demand for chatbots grows, creating isolated assistants becomes unsustainable. Our solution is an agent‑based architecture with a single entry point, MIA, which autonomously determines what each user can access based on their profile. By orchestrating specialized agents, MIA ensures scalability, consistency, and a more controlled, efficient user experience.

headings: The StockFish Project, Maintainer & Swiss National Supercomputing Centre Deputy Director
title: How AI will change the future of chess
icon_list_heading: Chess Master and Content Creator
text_editor_paragraphs: For decades, chess players have been terrified about the rise of computers and AI in the chess world. Many have believed that it will end the sport, because if computers are better than humans anyways, what is the point of having humans compete against eachother? This problem was famously raised in 1997 when the reigning world champion Garry Kasaprov lost to deepblue in a chess match. Today, that might seem like nothing unusual, but back then it symbolized that machines could excel at complex strategic tasks once thought to be uniquely human.

Although many still fear what the future of a sport characterized by human intelligence holds in a world of computers, it is crucial to understand how computers and AI are going to, and already have, revolutionized chess. In many ways, the understanding of this centuries old game, is reaching levels we never thought it could reach. Levels, that would not be possible to reach, without computers. To talk more about this, we have Joost VandeVondole here, who can explain further, how Stockfish completely revolutionized the chess world.

title: The Quantum Threat: Transitioning to Post-Quantum Security
text_editor_paragraphs: Quantum computing is becoming a structural threat to today’s cryptographic backbone. Shor’s algorithm breaks the public-key schemes that secure the web, communications, and cloud. Even before large-scale quantum machines arrive, “harvest now, decrypt later” makes the risk immediate. In this session, we connect the dots from cryptographic fundamentals to the real impact of quantum progress, then walk through the practical shift to post-quantum cryptography: NIST-standardized algorithms, hybrid migration patterns, and what integration looks like in telecom and cloud environments. We also introduce fully homomorphic encryption (FHE), enabling computation directly on encrypted data and closing the long-standing “data-in-use” security gap. We’ll wrap up with a live technical demonstration of encrypted data processing using FHE.

title: Hack Your CV: Key Tips to Optimize Your CV and Beat AI Filters

icon_list_heading: Chief Revenue Officer at Glide Identity

title: Building AI Agents with Spring AI
icon_list_heading: Organizer at Spring IO
text_editor_paragraphs: Building agents that actually work, ones that plan, reason, use tools, ask clarifying questions, and collaborate with other agents, requires more than just calling an LLM in a loop. It requires understanding agentic patterns and the protocols that connect them.

In this workshop, we’ll build progressively more capable AI agents using Spring AI. We’ll start from the fundamentals, interacting with LLMs, crafting prompts, and working with structured output. From there, we’ll explore Retrieval Augmented Generation (RAG) for grounding responses in your own data, and tool calling for giving models the ability to take action in the real world. With these foundations in place, we’ll move into agentic territory, looking at workflow patterns and then building autonomous agents with recursive advisors and composable skills. Finally, we’ll cover the emerging agent protocol landscape: MCP for connecting agents to external tools and services, and A2A for enabling agents to discover and collaborate with each other. You’ll leave with working code and a clear understanding of how to build production-ready agents with Spring AI.

title: From Prototype to Product: Operationalizing Generative Personalization at Scale
icon_list_heading: Research Scientist at Spotify
text_editor_paragraphs: Generative AI is reshaping personalization by making user experiences more expressive, intent-driven, and increasingly defined in natural language. Outputs become open-ended, contextual, and fine-grained, which disrupts much of the evaluation infrastructure that large-scale personalization has historically relied on. In these settings, “ground truth” is often underspecified, and quality depends on criteria like relevance, coherence, and user alignment rather than exact-match correctness. Yet human review and user studies do not scale. This talk explores LLM-as-a-Judge as a practical evaluation layer for generative and agentic systems. Drawing on recent research and public case studies, including published work from Spotify, we discuss how LLM-based judges can approximate human judgments, connect offline evaluation with human perception, and support meaningful assessment in low-feedback and cold-start settings. We then move from evaluation to optimization. When the judge signal is reliable, it can serve as an alignment signal for post-training and as a driver for automatic prompt and context optimization, enabling systematic comparisons, regression detection, and faster iteration cycles toward production-ready behavior.

In this talk, we will explore how agents can become the new cognitive microservices, capable of reasoning and acting within distributed systems. You’ll see demos with AWS serverless services that will help you understand how this new intelligent layer is transforming modern architecture.

headings: Global Head of Data & AI at Seidor
title: From “promstitution” to impact. How developers stay essential in the age of AI
icon_list_heading: Global Head of Data & AI at Seidor
text_editor_paragraphs: In a world where anyone can automate software generation with AI, code no longer makes the difference. When intelligent agents are within everyone’s reach, the real value lies in knowing what to do with them. In this session, we will analyze why delegating everything to AI is not the solution, discuss human agency, and share tips on what makes developers stand out so they can remain relevant and deliver greater value where AI cannot.

headings: Co-Founder at Future For Work Institute
title: NEXT HR – Market Intelligence for HR: HR Market Radar
icon_list_heading: CEO at TalentUp.io
text_editor_paragraphs: Market data has become a key factor in guiding digital talent management. This session will examine how the use of objective data and its interpretation enables informed decision-making in areas such as compensation, critical skills, talent location, and workforce planning, integrating a practical view of the current market while anticipating its future impact.

title: AI @ PAL Robots
icon_list_heading: Robotics Engineer at PAL Robotics
text_editor_paragraphs: This presentation explores how artificial intelligence is driving the development of humanoid robotics. We will examine the most widely used techniques and see how they are integrated into these systems, demonstrating how they enable robots to be more adaptive, safe, and collaborative. The session will also reflect on the potential of these technologies to improve daily life, assist with complex tasks, and expand human capabilities.

headings: EAE Business School - Master in Marketing & Growth Hacking Director
EAE Business School - Master in Marketing & Growth Hacking Director
title: AI Models for Marketing & Growth Strategy​
icon_list_heading: EAE Business School - Master in Marketing & Growth Hacking Director
text_editor_paragraphs: In this hands-on session, we will demonstrate how to use AI models to design and execute real marketing and growth strategies, regardless of experience level. Through clear examples and applied use cases, participants will learn how to select models, structure prompts, and build workflows that help attract, convert, and optimize business decisions. Applied AI you can start using from day one.

title: Computer Vision with Arduino UNO Q: From Edge AI to VLMs
text_editor_paragraphs: Join an interactive, hands-on workshop to explore Computer Vision on the edge using Edge Impulse and Arduino UNO Q. Learn how to collect data and train an object detection model to filter events in real time, then cascade to a Vision Language Model. No prior ML experience required. You’ll build an end-to-end cascaded AI application in the edge, balancing power efficiency and accuracy on real hardware. Bring your laptop and your curiosity!

title: How to train your robot
text_editor_paragraphs: 1. Asier Arranz– Senior Staff Developer Advocate atNVIDIA
2. Jesús Ángel Bravo Duque– Founder & CEO atBravo Robots
3. Johnny Nuñez– Developer Advocate at NVIDIA
4. Luis Bravo– Engineer at Bravo Robots

In this workshop, attendees will learn how to use the Lerobot framework and apply it to train a Lerobot SO-101 Arm. Official github: https://github.com/huggingface/lerobot/issues Buy the robot here: https://tienda.camptecnologico.com/producto/so-arm101-diy-kit-orange/

headings: Cloud Solutions Architect at Microsoft & Founder at Purplebook
title: From Adoption to Impact: How Adoption Accelerates AI & Cloud Transformation in Enterprise Teams
icon_list_heading: Cloud Solutions Architect at Microsoft & Founder at Purplebook
text_editor_paragraphs: Enterprises invest heavily in AI and cloud, yet struggle to realize value due to adoption gaps, not technology. This talk shares practical frameworks from enterprise and SaaS programs to improve onboarding, role-based training, and success metrics that accelerate adoption, reduce time-to-value, and drive sustained business impact.

title: What Does It Take to Build a Real-Time Data Platform?
icon_list_heading: Principal Product Manager at Clickhouse
text_editor_paragraphs: Modern AI systems—from recommendation engines to autonomous LLM agents—depend on fast, reliable access to fresh data. Feature retrieval, contextual enrichment, analytics, and observability all require sub-second responses, even at terabyte or petabyte scale, to deliver truly interactive experiences. In this session, we’ll explore how ClickHouse enables real-time analytics at scale and how it fits into end-to-end AI architectures.

headings: Sr. Solutions Architect Generative AI at NVIDIA
title: Building an AI Agent and Simulating 6G with NVIDIA
icon_list_heading: Sr. Solutions Architect Generative AI at NVIDIA

title: A More Accessible and Democratic Open-Source AI
text_editor_paragraphs: 1. Ángela Gasch (Moderator) – AI Consultant at SEDIA
2. Rebeca Sarai Gonzalez Guerra – Lead Data Scientist & AI Architect at Hiberus
3. Adriana Fernández – Head of the Telco Cloud & AI Innovation Team at OpenNebula
4. Alejandra González-García – Member of the Coordination Committee at Decidim

Panel discussion with organizations that develop, use, and advocate for open-source AI. We will discuss, from a technical and practical perspective, why open source is essential for accessible and transparent AI. A direct conversation about technology and how open source is shaping the future of AI.

title: Challenge Next Arena project presentations
text_editor_paragraphs: The objective of the challenge is to rethink Talent Arena for 2027. Imagine and design how this event should evolve in order to remain relevant, inspiring, and transformative. As part of the NextArena Challenge, several student teams will present the projects they have developed to define how Talent Arena can evolve to become the leading global forum for digital talent. Proposals should address different strategic areas, combining creativity and innovation with analysis and a forward-looking vision, while taking into account the objectives outlined above. Participants are expected to design an experience that connects with new generations, delivers value to senior professionals, drives innovation, and leaves a lasting impact on the European digital ecosystem. In this context, the challenge aims to open its strategy to innovative ideas and differentiated proposals for the 2027 edition, using the previously described elements as a reference and aligning with the strategic and future-oriented vision that is being defined.

title: NEXT HR –  Smart Hiring: AI-driven automation in tech talent recruitment
text_editor_paragraphs: Artificial intelligence is transforming digital talent recruitment processes. This session will explore the opportunities and challenges these tools present for HR teams, as well as their ethical and effective application. It will also examine the balance between technological efficiency, candidate experience, and human judgment, focusing on AI-augmented, people-centered decision-making.

headings: Computing Division Head at ALBA Synchrotron
HPC software research area director at BSC
title: Scaling Science at large facilities: ALBA and BSC
icon_list_heading: Computing Division Head at ALBA Synchrotron
text_editor_paragraphs: The Barcelona Supercomputing Center (BSC) and the ALBA Synchrotron are two major research hubs located in Barcelona. ALBA, a particle accelerator for experimental science, produces huge volumes of ultra-high-resolution data. BSC, the Spain’s national supercomputing center, hosts the MareNostrum and multiple research groups. This talk will examine the essential role of computing in driving the frontier of scientific discovery.

title: Immersive Interview Experience: Live a Real Interview
text_editor_paragraphs: The job interview is a demanding process that requires preparation and strategy. Through a hands-on, “training mode” experience, participants can practice, improve their pitch, and gain confidence to perform at their best in a real interview. In this workshop, you will find the most realistic simulator to help you avoid mistakes in an actual job interview.

headings: Head of Enterprise Artificial Intelligence Governance at AstraZeneca
title: The Future of Enterprise AI Governance
icon_list_heading: Head of Enterprise Artificial Intelligence Governance at AstraZeneca
text_editor_paragraphs: AI is now democratized, reshaping every role and organization. As it becomes embedded in work, how do we keep it trustworthy, compliant, and safe? Enterprise AI Governance is critical, yet most organizations are just beginning. Oliver Patel, who leads AI governance at AstraZeneca and authored Fundamentals of AI Governance, shares how regulations are evolving, why AI literacy is essential, and how agentic AI changes the game.

title: 20,000 Blunders in Tech Leadership
text_editor_paragraphs: The odyssey of the technical leader begins with a brilliant engineer who, after being called to cross the “technical career chasm,” must abandon their identity as a “chef in the kitchen” focused on code in order to learn how to achieve results through the hands of others. In this “choose your own adventure” labyrinth, our hero faces monsters such as technical debt, which they must learn to translate into the financial language of spreadsheets in order to fight it strategically.

Through the trials of the “You Build It, You Run It” philosophy and the demolition of organizational silos, they discover that their true strength lies in automation and shared product ownership. By invoking sacred tools such as “Priority Buckets” and OKRs to align their tribe with business objectives, and by applying the balm of blameless postmortems to transform mistakes into systemic learning, the leader returns transformed into the glue that binds technical talent with strategic vision.

Ultimately, the journey reveals that their mission is not only to plant technological “baby trees” for the future, but also to safeguard their own mental health and that of their team at the lonely summit of leadership. In this presentation, Àlex Rodríguez Bacardit and colleagues will discuss the complex path to technological leadership and share some of their greatest successes and failures.

title: Final Magamers
text_editor_paragraphs: Live broadcast space from the Gaming Zone at the Talent Arena, where casters Jackalsito and Katarsis will guide the audience through the on-site Grand Final of Magamers 2026, the women’s League of Legends tournament promoted by EL ESPAÑOL, Magas, Vandal, and Crónica Global, and integrated into the amateur competitive ecosystem of Circuito Tormenta.

Across three slots, the audience will be welcomed, the finalist teams will be introduced, and the different matches will be presented, providing context, key insights for each matchup, and live connections with the on-screen broadcast to experience the final from the inside.

title: NEXT HR – Rethinking Access to Digital Talent
icon_list_heading: Founder & CEO at Shakers
text_editor_paragraphs: Access to digital talent is evolving due to imbalances between profiles, increasing demand for specialization, and the prioritization of experienced profiles over the development of early-stage talent. This session will address new ways to identify and activate talent beyond traditional hiring, from early potential detection to flexible collaboration models, and their strategic implications for HR.

title: NEXT HR – Grow to Stay: Developing Digital Talent to Improve Retention
text_editor_paragraphs: Retaining digital talent is one of the main challenges facing HR teams today. Drawing on real-world experiences, this session will explore how professional development, upskilling, and continuous learning serve as key levers to strengthen engagement and retention. The focus will be on practical approaches that connect skills development with talent motivations and organizational needs.

title: The Power of Sharing: How Open Source Quietly Runs the World
icon_list_heading: Enterprise Architect at Allianz
text_editor_paragraphs: In this session, we will show how advocating OSS/Inner Source adoption at Allianz Technology. Using DORA + BaFin regulations as catalyst, OpenSource adoption can turn compliance into a competitive advantage. With a data-driven approach, implementing an Open Source Program Office (OSPO) drives immediate ROI (44-60% cost savings, 25-40% faster delivery).

title: Tapas & Observability: Connect, Chat, Enjoy
icon_list_heading: Principal Software Engineer at Dynatrace

title: Management and leadership: STEM intergeneracional
text_editor_paragraphs: 1. Amaia Zarranz – Technical Solution – Caixabank Tech
2. Maria Engracia Guillamon Bagan – Directora Cibersecurity Prevent&Detect – Caixabank
3. Claudia Platel – Product Manager Analítica i venta digital – Caixabank
4. Lourdes Mercadal – Directora Development – CaixaBank Tech

text_editor_paragraphs: 1. Humbert Ruiz (Moderator) – Director of Digital Talent atBarcelona Activa
2. Xavier Massa – Director, Public Sector Spain atCISCO
