Presentation of the Cybersecurity track of the IT Academy. This roundtable will explain the public-private collaboration that makes this training possible, designed byBarcelona Activa, with the participation ofCISCO, which provides students access to its Cisco Networking Academy, and SIRT, a leading cybersecurity company that facilitates official student certification. The event will also feature the presence ofINCIBE.

title: Coming Soon Ajuntament de Barcelona
text_editor_paragraphs: Coming Soon Ajuntament de Barcelona

title: PUCRA: University Students Competing in High-Level Robotics
text_editor_paragraphs: 1. Elena Villalba Aguilera– ERC Team Leader atPUCRA
2. Santiago Pallarès Ocampo– President of PUCRA
3. Marc Redolad Ramos– Treasurer of PUCRA

PUCRA is a university association formed by engineering students who design and build robots for national and international competitions. This session presents the team’s experience in the VEX robotics competition, the SENER competition and the return to the European Rover Challenge (ERC) competition, explaining the rules, technical challenges, and current project status.

title: Invisible Technology
icon_list_heading: CEO at NutriBrain
text_editor_paragraphs: Twenty years ago, technology was noisy: beeping modems, loading times, instruction manuals. Today, the goal is “Zero UI”: technology that is everywhere, but invisible. What does it take to achieve that invisibility? It requires projecting 30,000 invisible points of light onto your face, synchronizing atomic clocks in orbit, and embedding microscopic physical machines inside a chip. In this session, we will examine the technical miracles that users take for granted, and how technology performs magic in our everyday lives.

title: AI Lift-off: Escaping Prototype Gravity to Reach Production Orbit
icon_list_heading: Solutions Architect at Databricks
text_editor_paragraphs: Most AI-powered prototypes never make it to production, but why? In this session, we’ll explore the hidden forces that stall AI projects, from fragile data foundations and lack of unified governance to prioritizing the never-ending chase of the ‘best’ model over rigorous evaluation and actionable data intelligence. Learn how enterprises are overcoming these hurdles to turn experiments into production-ready AI that delivers real impact – no spacesuits required!

headings: Global Leadership and Talent Analytics at Unilever
title: NEXT HR – Data-Driven People: Analytics per decisions de talent
icon_list_heading: Global Leadership and Talent Analytics at Unilever
text_editor_paragraphs: Decisions on how to manage and develop talent are no longer linear, and HR analytics has become a key factor in strengthening them. Using real-world examples, this session will show how People Analytics can improve retention, performance, the identification of development needs, and workforce planning, connecting people data with actionable business decisions for everyday HR operations.

headings: Master in Supply Chain Director, EAE Business School
title: AI in Supply Chain/Operations: How to Deliver More Value in an Unstable World
icon_list_heading: Master in Supply Chain Director, EAE Business School
text_editor_paragraphs: Practical workshop for undergraduate/graduate students, active professionals, and anyone looking to start a career in Operations/Supply Chain (including Purchasing, Procurement, Production, Logistics, Transportation, Demand & Supply Planning, Customer Service).

Through individual exercises and small team challenges, you will discover how to turn AI into a real competitive advantage: you will identify high-impact opportunities, learn to prioritize them with strategic judgment, and transform them into measurable business results as well as a direct accelerator for your own career, working on prompting through practical exercises applied to real situations.
We will close the workshop by creating a 30-day individual plan to enhance your current and future employability based on the AI experiences, in an increasingly uncertain and unstable global context.
Important: Bring your laptop with your preferred AI agent installed/connected.

title: Build Your Own AI Agent – From Theory to Practice
text_editor_paragraphs: In this workshop, you will discover the theoretical foundations behind AI agents and learn, step by step, how to develop and customize them. You will experience a hands-on, interactive session, working with real examples to fine-tune your agents’ performance. No prior experience is needed—just curiosity, a willingness to learn, and a desire to explore new ways of working with artificial intelligence.

headings: Sr. Director and Head of Aerial Product Management
title: How to Simulate 6G in the Cloud with Highly-Realistic Channel Models
icon_list_heading: Sr. Director and Head of Aerial Product Management
text_editor_paragraphs: Wireless network digital twins are key to AI-native 6G networks, which will be born first in simulation. Legacy stochastic models face fundamental challenges in physical accuracy and ISAC needs. NVIDIA Aerial Omniverse Digital Twin (AODT) employs GPU raytracing for physics-accurate 6G modeling. In this session, we will show how to integrate AODT into simulation workflows, enabling cloud-based AI-for-RAN validation and realistic network deployment

title: Hack Your CV: Key Tips to Optimize Your CV and Beat AI Filters
text_editor_paragraphs: In today’s recruitment processes, many résumés are automatically filtered before reaching human hands. Understanding how these systems work is key to improving job opportunities. This workshop provides practical tools and strategies to adapt your CV to AI filters, aiming especially to empower unemployed individuals, people with diverse profiles, or those in vulnerable situations.

headings: Head of People at Mobile World Capital Barcelona
title: NEXT HR – Closing
icon_list_heading: Head of People at Mobile World Capital Barcelona
text_editor_paragraphs: Coming Soon NEXT HR

headings: Senior AI Manager and Principal AI Architect at HP
title: AI That Ships: Powering the Next Wave of AI PCs with Generative AI at the Edge
icon_list_heading: Senior AI Manager and Principal AI Architect at HP
text_editor_paragraphs: Building AI demos is easy; shipping AI products is hard. This talk shows how HP is unleashing GenAI on next-gen AI PCs, delivering powerful, secure, private, and energy-efficient on-device intelligence. Explore multimodal models optimized via fine-tuning, quantization, hybrid CPU/GPU/NPU orchestration, beyond transformer-based architectures like state-space models, and advanced assessment frameworks shaping next-gen AI experiences.

title: AI Across the Pharmaceutical Value Chain
icon_list_heading: Data Scientist R&D Lead
text_editor_paragraphs: The pharmaceutical industry is at a turning point, with long, costly drug development cycles. At Sanofi Digital R&D, AI is transforming the full value chain — from molecular design and advanced imaging to clinical trials and digital biomarkers. By accelerating decisions and improving outcomes, AI is reimagining how medicines are discovered and developed.

headings: Global Rust Community Coordinator at Rust
title: Rust Iberia Meetup: Building an Agent from Scratch Using Rust and Open‑Source Tools
icon_list_heading: Founder/Instructor at Fledgio

title: Final Magamers
text_editor_paragraphs: Live broadcast space from the Gaming Zone at the Talent Arena, where casters Jackalsito and Katarsis will guide the audience through the on-site Grand Final of Magamers 2026, the women’s League of Legends tournament promoted by EL ESPAÑOL, Magas, Vandal, and Crónica Global, and integrated into the amateur competitive ecosystem of Circuito Tormenta.

Across three slots, the audience will be welcomed, the finalist teams will be introduced, and the different matches will be presented, providing context, key insights for each matchup, and live connections with the on-screen broadcast to experience the final from the inside.

title: Live Coding: Create your first Voice Agent
icon_list_heading: SLNG.ai - Co-founder & CTO
text_editor_paragraphs: In this live-coding session, we’ll build a simple voice agent step by step: handling audio, reasoning with an LLM, and calling tools in real time (fetching web data, reading from PDFs, triggering actions). No slides—just code. You’ll leave with a clear mental model of how voice agents work and how to extend them beyond scripted conversations.

title: AI with Purpose: Ethics that Transform Talent Management
icon_list_heading: CEO of Eurofirms
text_editor_paragraphs: Eurofirms Group and Telefónica Tech share a success story of purpose-driven innovation: how generative AI, designed around values, is transforming talent selection with fairer, scalable and more human processes, where technology supports and human decision-making remains in control. In addition, you will discover the tech trends that will redefine the relationship between talent and technology in the coming years.

title: Building the AI Generation. Empowering Research Talent
text_editor_paragraphs: TheBuilding the AI Generationinitiative, part ofGeneración Dand promoted byRed.estogether withCSIC,Barcelona Supercomputing Center,CNIOandCNIC, has enabled the recruitment of 374 researchers in artificial intelligence and digitalization applied to multiple scientific fields.
In this panel, representatives and researchers from CSIC and CNIC will discuss the evolution of the program and present some of the most relevant research projects currently under development.

title: Responsible and Secure AI: Protect Your Organization in the Era of Generative AI
text_editor_paragraphs: 1. Nadya Lozada Oliveros – Project Manager / Cloud Architect at GFT (Moderator)
2. Miguel Fernández Garrido – Co-Head of the Cybersecurity CoE at GFT Spain
3. Eduardo Gonzalez Lainez – Information Security Director at Banc Sabadell
4. Alex Jiménez de Alier – Head of Cyber Prevention at Boehringer Ingelheim

Generative AI is transforming organizations, enabling efficiency, automation and new ways of building software. However, major risks remain, including data leakage, model attacks, hallucinations, AI lifecycle security, regulatory compliance and ethical challenges. In this roundtable, experts from GFT and industry leaders share practical guidance on responsible implementation, security by design and safe use of generative AI in production.

title: The anticulture of urgency: How to avoid the immediate-response syndrome
icon_list_heading: Scrum Master at Wordline
text_editor_paragraphs: Many IT teams live in a constant sense of urgency: messages in Teams that demand immediate responses, emails that can’t wait, emergency meetings. But is it really all that urgent? This talk will address how the pressure of immediacy affects productivity and team mental health, and present strategies for slowing down without losing efficiency. We will look at some techniques and how to apply them in our day-to-day work.

headings: Platform Area Lead and Cloud Security Advocate at Tymit
title: Leading a Technical Revolution through Platform Engineering
icon_list_heading: Platform Area Lead and Cloud Security Advocate at Tymit
text_editor_paragraphs: Technical revolutions rarely start with big launches or visible announcements. More often, they begin deep within an organization — where architectures are defined, processes are automated, and decisions are made that few notice but ultimately shape the company’s speed, security, and capacity to grow. In this talk, Anastasia Kondratieva shares what it really means to lead that kind of transformation through Platform Engineering, combining strategy with hands-on technical execution. Through real examples —evolving infrastructure, designing internal platforms, making governance and automation decisions— she explores how the often invisible work of platform teams can become a tangible driver of organizational change. The session also looks at how to align technology with culture and maintain coherence as an organization scales and evolves, showing that a well-executed technical revolution is not built on chaos but on clarity — on decisions that scale, automations that endure, and platforms that propel the entire company forward. Key takeaways: How architectural and technical decisions can drive organizational transformation. Platform Engineering as both a technical and strategic engine of change. Real examples of platform design, automation, and governance. How to align technology, culture, and growth. Scaling without losing control: balancing autonomy, security, and sustainability.

headings: Software Engineer and Educator at MoureDev
title: How to Survive an AI That’s Trying to Replace You
icon_list_heading: Software Engineer and Educator at MoureDev
text_editor_paragraphs: Brais Moure deconstructs the myth of the “dying programmer” to focus on the shift from writing code to designing solutions. Discover why engineering fundamentals are more critical than ever and how to leverage automation without losing technical control. Key Takeaways: The evolving value of the modern developer. Navigating the risks of automated coding. Scaling your career with technical rigor.

title: AI Observability: Making AI Adoption Safe, Reliable, and Measurable
icon_list_heading: Senior Product Manager at Dynatrace
text_editor_paragraphs: This session unpacks the real challenges organizations face when adopting AI—from governance and transparency to performance, reliability, and cost. We’ll break down why traditional monitoring falls short in AI-driven environments and how AI observability becomes essential for trust, reliability, and scale. Finally, we’ll show how Dynatrace delivers end‑to‑end visibility across the entire AI lifecycle—from development and testing to production.

headings: Country Leader Spain at Eurofirms Group
Business Leader Claire Joster Spain at Claire Joster
title: Meetup with Eurofirms
icon_list_heading: Country Leader Spain at Eurofirms Group

headings: Co-founder at Patrones y Escondites S.L.
title: What I Would Tell Myself 8 Years Ago Before Founding an Indie Studio
icon_list_heading: Co-founder at Patrones y Escondites S.L.
text_editor_paragraphs: Eight years ago, we founded an indie studio convinced that making good games was the hardest part. Over time, we realized that wasn’t exactly the case. In this session, I will share what we’ve learned along the way: the small decisions that made unexpected differences, the conversations we took too long to have, and the mistakes we made in the games we released.

We will talk about teams, contracts, publishers, marketing, and something as unglamorous as cash flow and reach. Not from theory or absolute certainty, but from our own experience, with its successes and its missteps.

This is not a talk about what must be done. It’s an open reflection on what we wish we had understood more clearly when we were starting out. Because making a game is a creative challenge. Sustaining a studio over time is something you learn, often through trial and error.

title: Testing APIs Like a Boss with BDD
icon_list_heading: Solutions Architect at Next Digital
text_editor_paragraphs: A hands-on workshop on API testing in Python using TDD and BDD to build robust, maintainable software. We’ll cover key concepts and apply them with pytest, pytest-bdd, and a dockerized environment, writing tests before code, defining natural-language scenarios, and automating validation. For developers with basic Python and FastAPI knowledge.

title: Sovereign AI & FinOps: Turning Cloud Control into Competitive Advantage
icon_list_heading: CEO at Digital Maker
text_editor_paragraphs: As AI costs rise, organizations must innovate without losing financial control. This workshop explores how FinOps enables Sovereign AI by combining financial governance with data residency. Through real-world cases, we demonstrate how to reduce cloud waste and re-architect for efficiency. Join us to learn how to align innovation, profitability, and sovereignty to build resilient, future-ready digital ecosystems.

title: Building and Training Open Telco AI Models at Scale
icon_list_heading: Data Scientist at AT&T
text_editor_paragraphs: This masterclass establishes a technical framework for building and training telecom-native AI systems at scale. It details the end-to-end infrastructure required to transition from general-purpose models to domain-specific architectures using ScalarLM. Attendees will see a live demonstration of multi-node training on open Telco AI assets and explore methods for achieving state-of-the-art performance on industry benchmarks.

title: CTO Leadership Lab: Communicate, Attract, and Retain Tech Talent
text_editor_paragraphs: 1. Sandra Alonso – Program Leader at Eurofirms
2. Marisa Martínez – Diversity & Inclusion Consultant at Eurofirms
3. Mireia López – Diversity & Inclusion Consultant at Eurofirms

title: From Devices to Digital: Building Healthcare Workflows that Matter
icon_list_heading: Innovation IT Manager at BBraun
text_editor_paragraphs: Healthcare is moving beyond devices to integrated digital solutions. This session explores how combining medical technology with software, cloud, and data can simplify hospital workflows, reduce friction for clinicians, and improve patient care—turning technology into a true enabler of efficiency and impact.

headings: Global Insights and Thought Leadership at Harvard Business Review
title: Now and Next
icon_list_heading: Global Insights and Thought Leadership at Harvard Business Review
text_editor_paragraphs: “Now and Next” frames how organizations and professionals balance today’s urgent needs with a future shaped by AI. With major skill shifts ahead and limited training, AI’s impact depends on investing in people through reskilling, redesigned work, and human‑centered cultures. The “Now” is running today’s business; the “Next” demands leadership to ensure AI augments human capability and drives inclusive growth.

headings: Director of Postgraduate Studies in Entrepreneurship and Startup Creation
title: Rebuild AI: Generative AI for Business (Live Demos)
icon_list_heading: Director of Postgraduate Studies in Entrepreneurship and Startup Creation
text_editor_paragraphs: Generative AI is no longer a trend—it’s competitive infrastructure. In this session, with live demonstrations, we will see how to integrate AI into real business processes to multiply productivity, accelerate decision-making, and create functional solutions without coding. Real cases. Immediate impact. Applicable from day one.

title: Without talent there is no technology: Catalonia’s digital future
text_editor_paragraphs: The roundtable will focus on the shortage of digital talent in Catalonia, currently the main barrier to the growth and competitiveness of the ICT sector. We will address this challenge from a cross-cutting perspective: that of the technology sector, through the experience of 3Cat, and that of public policy, with the participation of the Secretariat for Digital Policies and the Secretariat for Equality and Feminism.

title: AI and central banking
text_editor_paragraphs: The panel examines how AI is redefining the future of central banking, from enhancing data-driven decision-making to strengthening financial stability frameworks. As AI is increasingly applied to economic forecasting and supervisory analysis, central banks face both unprecedented opportunities and new governance challenges. The discussion will explore emerging use cases.

title: Coming Soon SEDIA
text_editor_paragraphs: Coming Soon SEDIA

headings: SOMNI GAME STUDIOSCEO & Co-Founder & Lead Game Designer
title: How to Live 150 Years Through Gaming Biotech
icon_list_heading: SOMNI GAME STUDIOSCEO & Co-Founder & Lead Game Designer
text_editor_paragraphs: Can a Gaming studio help people live 150 years with a biological age of 30?
Can a game do it?How do you build a video game studio like a biotech startup?

In this talk, we will address these questions from the very beginning of the idea, the experience, and the decisions we had to make. How it emerged, how it evolved, and how we financed a studio with an unusual mission in the industry: using gaming to directly impact human health and longevity.

Today we know that up to 80% of our life expectancy and quality of life depends on modifiable habits such as movement, strength, rest, or nutrition. However, most solutions fail due to lack of adherence. The problem is not information, it is behavior.

Based on that premise, we structured the studio as a startup: strategic funding, clear narrative, complementary team, and impact metrics, while trying to maintain art and storytelling as differentiating elements.
It is not just about creating a game, but about designing systems that help people live 150 years with a biological age of 30.

headings: VP Engineering for Accounting products at SAGE
title: Cybersecurity during the product and AI development process
icon_list_heading: VP Engineering for Accounting products at SAGE
text_editor_paragraphs: In the context of a big software product company (11.000 employees), this presentation describes how the SDLC is evolving to add security-by-design principles for modern product development with of AI for internal and external usage. Key areas include: secure coding practices, the role of the security architects and DevSecOps automation. Special focus on AI-specific risks: data poisoning, prompt injection, model theft, and training data privacy. Emphasizes building security culture in software engineering teams.

headings: VP Global Data Science and Advanced Analytics at Pepsico
title: The AI ROI Trap
icon_list_heading: VP Global Data Science and Advanced Analytics at Pepsico
text_editor_paragraphs: Most organizations measure AI ROI through efficiency—hours saved or tasks automated—but these rarely reflect real business impact. This session reframes ROI around velocity, decision flow, reliability, and impact. We’ll discuss why demos impress but don’t scale, how generative and agentic AI fit long-term progress, and why deployment requires balancing innovation risk and reward.

title: Hack Your Interview: Face Interviews with Confidence and Authenticity
text_editor_paragraphs: In a tech sector where cognitive diversity drives innovation, approaching interviews with authenticity is key. Neurodiversity adds value to the recruitment process. This workshop provides practical tools for managing interviews and communication with confidence, helping neurodivergent and diverse individuals showcase their talent, strengthen self-awareness, and enter the tech sector with greater assurance.

title: Profitable, Pragmatic, and Still Standing: Building a €130M Revenue Fintech by Being Contrarian
icon_list_heading: CTO at SeQura
text_editor_paragraphs: Monolith when everyone screamed microservices. Profitable when startups were supposed to burn. Small teams when scale demanded process. For years, being contrarian wasn’t just working at seQura, it was our advantage. Then we hit 100 engineers and the rebellions that saved us started killing us. This is the story of what we got right by ignoring best practices, what we got catastrophically wrong, and the harder lesson: it’s not about being a rebel or a conformist. It’s about knowing which rules to break and which ones will break you.

headings: Head of Clinical Data Management at Bayer
Head of Digital Health and Real World Science at Bayer
title: Improving patients’ life through artificial intelligence in pharma
icon_list_heading: Head of Clinical Data Management at Bayer
text_editor_paragraphs: Patients often face disconnection from their expectations and the reality of their clinical experience. Artificial intelligence (AI) offers promising solutions focused on optimizing drug discovery and streamlining clinical trials. AI can help us to bridge the gap, leading to more patient-centric healthcare.

title: How to train your robot
icon_list_heading: Founder & CEO at Bravo Robots
text_editor_paragraphs: The Generative AI revolution has also reached robotics. World and VLC (Vision, Language, Action) models applied to new robotic systems equipped with powerful computing systems allow non-deterministic tasks to be solved. It is time to train robots. This workshop will discuss the latest developments in embedded AI and how the robotics industry is responding.

headings: Digital & AI Business Director at Roca Salvatella
title: From digital to agentic (a realistic approximation)
icon_list_heading: Digital & AI Business Director at Roca Salvatella
text_editor_paragraphs: This isn’t a talk about tech promises; it’s about real-world execution. For leaders ready to move beyond basic digitization, we will break down the 10 strategic steps essential for efficient AI deployment. We will explore what the shift toward “agentic” workflows actually looks like through practical examples that bridge the gap between ambition and measurable impact. You will discover how to align business goals with human talent, avoiding the common traps that stall adoption. If you want a clear mental framework to make better decisions, prioritize investment, and lead your team with realism, this session provides the roadmap you can apply the next day.

headings: Chief Technology Officer & Digital Innovation Director at ESIC University
title: Exponential Leadership: Managing High-Performance IT Teams in the GenAI Era.
icon_list_heading: Chief Technology Officer & Digital Innovation Director at ESIC University
text_editor_paragraphs: AI is redefining IT leadership. Discover how to adapt roles, foster Human-AI collaboration, and lead cultural transformation. We will explore strategies to optimize productivity, turn AI into a competitive advantage, and ensure your IT team stays at the forefront of innovation. Prepare your team for the future!

headings: Associate Director - Software & Platform Engineering at Codemotion
title: Our AI Agents Are Finally In Production — And Now What?
icon_list_heading: Associate Director - Software & Platform Engineering at Codemotion
text_editor_paragraphs: Agentic AIs are driving organizations to reinvent the way they work but scaling from a few agents to thousands is not a simple endeavor. From the strong foundations of platform engineering principles to model gateways. From AI agent pipelines to continuous evaluation of agents. From agent to agent interactions to tool integrations. The “Swiss army knife” of AI-intensive platforms to cover what “I wish I had known six months ago.

title: How Playgami Powers the Next Generation of Games
icon_list_heading: CTO, Playgami at Scopely
text_editor_paragraphs: Playgami, Scopely’s technology platform for game operations, has powered some of the world’s most successful mobile games for over a decade. Despite its 12-year history, it still feels modern, adaptable, and ahead of the industry curve. The session explores the software development principles and architectural choices behind its long-term evolution, resilience, and speed, and how close collaboration with internal studios and product teams shaped its roadmap and driven adoption across Scopely.

headings: Lecturer on the Master's Degree in Marketing, Digital and E-commerce Barcelona at EAE Business School
title: Leadership When Machines Also Decide
icon_list_heading: Lecturer on the Master's Degree in Marketing, Digital and E-commerce Barcelona at EAE Business School
text_editor_paragraphs: Artificial intelligence not only accelerates processes—it is redefining how decisions are made in organizations. Systems that analyze, recommend, and execute now share the space of human judgment. In this context, leadership means designing robust decision-making frameworks, governing judgment, and taking responsibility in hybrid environments. We will explore which competencies will make the difference and why value will lie in strategic judgment.

title: From Disruption to Adoption, The Secure GPT Journey
icon_list_heading: Software Engineer Lead at AXA
text_editor_paragraphs: Discover AXA’s transformative journey with Secure GPT: a robust GenAI platform for enterprise safety, scalability, and innovation. Secure GPT offers a user-friendly web interface and a secure API gateway, enabling seamless GenAI use in daily work. Learn how iterative adoption, federated collaboration, and continuous upskilling are driving Secure GPT’s evolution—empowering AXA employees worldwide to innovate securely and efficiently.

title: Magamers Award Ceremony
text_editor_paragraphs: Closing ceremony of the Magamers by Santander 2026 women’s tournament, where the best moments of the competition will be revisited and female talent in esports will be highlighted. The event will be hosted by Pablo Grandío (Vandal and Quincemil) and will feature contributions from Cruz Sánchez de Lara, Óscar “Fosky” Compadre, and José Sánchez Montero (FNAC), who will reflect on the role of women in the video game industry. The ceremony will conclude with the presentation of trophies and prize checks to the champion and runner-up teams, followed by the official closing of the tournament.

title: 10 lessons I would have said to the me of 25 years ago
icon_list_heading: CEO at Ogilvy Spain
text_editor_paragraphs: We live in a time of unprecedented transformation. Artificial Intelligence is redefining the rules of the game, and in this environment, adaptability and strategic vision are more crucial than ever. Throughout my career, I have learned that success is not only measured in results, but in the ability to inspire, innovate and lead in uncertainty. I want to share with you 10 principles that I consider fundamental to thriving in this dynamic environment. These reflections, based on my personal experience, will provide you with practical guidance for meeting challenges, embracing innovation and achieving sustainable success. Together, we will explore how to transform disruption into opportunity and build a more prosperous future for all.

title: CTF Awards
Decode Yourself: Discover Your DISC Style and Improve How You Work
text_editor_paragraphs: The DISC model identifies four behavioral styles that influence how we make decisions, communicate, and face challenges. Understanding them improves teamwork, professional effectiveness, and interpersonal relationships, helping individuals gain better self-awareness and respond appropriately in any situation. A workshop designed to help participants decode themselves through DISC.

title: Day One: Shooting the Future — AI, VFX, and the Freedom Dilemma
text_editor_paragraphs: 1. Jordi Mollà – Actor
2. Cristina Pons – Executive Producer at Grupo iZen
3. José Velasco – CEO of PLIA
4. Laia Corbella – Director of Communications and Institutional Relations at Mobile World Capital
Presentation ofDay One, an original Spanish tech thriller on Prime Video (premiering March 2026), starring Álex González and Jordi Mollá, set in Barcelona during the Mobile World Congress.
In this talk, the creative and production team will show how AI applied to VFX was integrated into filming to generate imaginary technology, interfaces, and high-production-value shots.
Barcelona—with landmarks such as the MWC, the BSC, and Torre Glòries—becomes the protagonist of a story that explores the moral dilemmas of technological development.
The series poses an essential question: if technology can rewrite emotions, would you choose to be happy… or free?

headings: Principal SRE at SCRM Lidl International Hub
title: Operating the Heart of the Sovereign Cloud
icon_list_heading: Principal SRE at SCRM Lidl International Hub
text_editor_paragraphs: Running an IdP at scale allows no room for error: if identity fails, the cloud goes down. In this technical talk, we open the hood of STACKIT to show how we manage our critical core. We’ll walk through our GitOps workflow, how we implement canary rollouts protected by automated end-to-end tests, and how we minimize the blast radius of every deployment. Real resilience engineering for the European cloud—no marketing slides.
